name: TVS Apache RTR 200 4V
text: TVS Apache RTR 200 4V is a sports bike. It has 197.0cc engine, gives 38 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.47 Lakh. The TVS Apache RTR 200 4V was originally launched in India in early 2016 as a flagship streetfighter. It features a 197.75cc oil-cooled engine that produces approximately 20.8 PS of power. The bike introduced first-in-segment features like multiple riding modes to adjust power delivery. It comes equipped with SmartXonnect technology for smartphone pairing and navigation. The mechanical setup includes a race-tuned slipper clutch for smoother downshifts. For safety, it utilizes a dual-channel ABS system with Rear-wheel Lift-off Protection. The aggressive design is highlighted by a claw-styled LED headlamp and a signature double-barrel exhaust. It remains a top choice for riders seeking a balanced performance commuter for city and track use.

name: TVS Apache RR 310
text: TVS Apache RR 310 is a super-sport bike. It has 312.0cc engine, gives 33 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 2.72 Lakh. The Apache RR 310 was launched in 2017 as the first product of the TVS-BMW collaboration. It shares its reverse-inclined DOHC engine with the BMW G 310 R. The bike features a distinctive shark-inspired fairing that offers superior aerodynamics. In 2024, it was updated with aero-winglets and a bi-directional quickshifter. The trellis frame provides exceptional rigidity and handling precision on the racetrack. It includes a vertical 5-inch TFT instrument cluster with comprehensive data logging. Riders can choose between four riding modes: Track, Sport, Urban, and Rain. It is widely considered the most versatile entry-level supersport motorcycle in India today.

name: TVS Ronin
text: TVS Ronin is a scrambler bike. It has 225.0cc engine, gives 42 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.49 Lakh. TVS introduced the Ronin in 2022 as a unique lifestyle motorcycle that blends cruiser and scrambler traits. It is powered by a 225.9cc oil-cooled engine designed specifically for strong low-end torque. The bike features a square-stroke engine configuration that ensures smooth city rideability. It comes equipped with gold-finished USD forks and a signature "T" shaped LED DRL. The Ronin features dual-channel ABS with switchable modes for Urban and Rain conditions. Technology highlights include voice assistance and turn-by-turn navigation via a circular digital console. It features an offset instrument cluster which adds to its neo-retro aesthetic appeal. This motorcycle targets riders looking for a premium, unscripted experience for both city and highway.

name: TVS Raider 125
text: TVS Raider 125 is a commuter bike. It has 124.0cc engine, gives 67 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.95 Lakh. The TVS Raider 125 was launched in 2021 to redefine the premium 125cc commuter segment. It features a modern 124.8cc 3-valve engine that produces class-leading power and torque. The bike is equipped with an "Eco" and "Power" riding mode, a first for its segment. It boasts a mono-shock rear suspension which is rarely seen in this displacement category. The styling is sharp and aggressive, featuring a unique LED headlamp and a muscular fuel tank. A full-color TFT display is available in the top variant, offering Bluetooth connectivity. It also includes an under-seat storage space for small essentials like smartphones. This bike is popular among younger buyers for its combination of style, features, and fuel economy.

name: TVS Apache RTR 160 4V
text: TVS Apache RTR 160 4V is a sports bike. It has 159.0cc engine, gives 45 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.24 Lakh. Launched in 2018, the Apache RTR 160 4V set a new benchmark for performance in the 160cc class. It uses a 159.7cc oil-cooled engine with 4-valve technology for better high-rev performance. The bike delivers approximately 17.55 PS, making it one of the most powerful in its segment. It features a "Double Cradle Split Synchro Stiff Chassis" for improved handling and stability. TVS updated the bike recently with riding modes and an integrated LED headlamp. The Urban and Rain modes limit top speed and adjust ABS intervention for safety. It features a wide radial rear tire that provides excellent grip during cornering. This model is often cited as the best all-rounder for enthusiasts on a budget.

name: Honda CB350 H'ness
text: Honda CB350 H'ness is a cruiser bike. It has 348.0cc engine, gives 35 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 2.1 Lakh. Honda launched the H'ness CB350 in India in September 2020 to enter the modern-classic segment. It is powered by a 348.36cc air-cooled, single-cylinder engine that delivers 30 Nm of torque. The engine is noted for its refined performance and a distinctive "thump" that appeals to cruiser fans. It features an Assist and Slipper clutch that reduces the effort required to operate the lever. Safety is handled by a dual-channel ABS system and Honda Selectable Torque Control. The bike includes a semi-digital instrument cluster with Bluetooth connectivity and voice assist. Its retro-inspired design is complemented by full LED lighting and premium chrome finishes. It offers a relaxed riding triangle, making it ideal for long-distance highway touring.

name: Honda Hornet 2.0
text: Honda Hornet 2.0 is a sports bike. It has 184.0cc engine, gives 45 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.39 Lakh. The Honda Hornet 2.0 was introduced in 2020 as a replacement for the older CB Hornet 160R. It features a larger 184.4cc engine that produces 17.2 PS and 16.1 Nm of torque. A major highlight of this bike is the gold-finished upside-down (USD) front forks. The design is muscular and street-focused, with a new X-shaped LED tail lamp. It offers a fully digital liquid crystal instrument cluster with 5 levels of brightness adjustment. The bike is equipped with a petal disc brake setup and single-channel ABS for safety. It features a sporty split-seat setup and an engine stop-start switch for convenience. This motorcycle is designed for riders who want a premium street-naked experience for city commutes.

name: Honda CB300R
text: Honda CB300R is a neo-sports bike. It has 286.0cc engine, gives 30 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 2.4 Lakh. The Honda CB300R was first launched in India in 2019 as a "Neo Sports CafÃ©" inspired machine. It is powered by a 286cc liquid-cooled DOHC engine that produces 31 PS of power. The bike is exceptionally lightweight at just 146 kg, providing a great power-to-weight ratio. It features a minimalist design with a round LED headlamp and exposed trellis frame. The hardware includes 41cc USD forks and an IMU-based dual-channel ABS system. A full digital instrument cluster provides all necessary information, including a gear position indicator. It utilizes an Assist and Slipper clutch for effortless gear changes during aggressive downshifts. This bike is perfect for urban riders who appreciate premium build quality and agile handling.

name: Honda Shine 125
text: Honda Shine 125 is a commuter bike. It has 124.0cc engine, gives 55 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.8 Lakh. The Honda Shine 125 was launched in 2006 and has become Indiaâ€™s best-selling 125cc motorcycle. It features a reliable 123.9cc air-cooled engine equipped with Hondaâ€™s eSP technology. The engine is known for its silent start feature and exceptionally smooth power delivery. It offers a comfortable long seat and a 5-step adjustable rear suspension for ride comfort. The bike is designed for daily commuters who prioritize durability and low maintenance costs. It features a simple analog console that is easy to read under direct sunlight. The Shine includes a Combi-Brake System (CBS) with equalizer for balanced braking performance. It continues to be the preferred choice for families and office-goers across India.

name: Hero Xtreme 160R 4V
text: Hero Xtreme 160R 4V is a sports bike. It has 163.0cc engine, gives 45 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.39 Lakh. The Hero Xtreme 160R 4V was introduced in 2023 as a performance upgrade to the 2V model.The Hero Xtreme 160R 4V is a performance-oriented street motorcycle designed for the youth in the Indian market. It is powered by a 163.2cc 4-valve oil-cooled engine that generates a maximum power of 16.9 PS and a peak torque of 14.6 Nm. The bike is capable of achieving a top speed of 115 kmph, providing a spirited performance in city traffic. It features premium 37mm KYB upside-down (USD) front forks for superior handling and stability. The motorcycle comes with a sleek LED headlamp and a sharp, aerodynamic fuel tank design. It is equipped with a digital instrument cluster that offers call and SMS alerts via Bluetooth. The safety is ensured by a single-channel ABS and petal disc brakes on both ends. Its lightweight chassis makes it highly maneuverable, with a 0-60 kmph sprint time of just 4.41 seconds. This model is currently a flagship in Hero's premium commuter segment.

name: Hero Karizma XMR
text: Hero Karizma XMR is a sports bike. It has 210.0cc engine, gives 35 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.8 Lakh. Hero revived the iconic Karizma nameplate in 2023 with the launch of the Karizma XMR. It is the first Hero motorcycle to feature a liquid-cooled, DOHC, 4-valve engine. The 210cc engine delivers 25.5 PS of power and 20.4 Nm of torque. It comes with a 6-speed gearbox and an assist and slipper clutch as standard. The bike features a segment-first adjustable windscreen for better wind protection on highways. Its design is sharp and modern, featuring an intelligent LED headlamp that activates automatically. A full-digital instrument cluster offers turn-by-turn navigation and smartphone alerts via Bluetooth. This bike targets enthusiasts who grew up with the Karizma and want modern performance.

name: Hero Xpulse 200 4V
text: Hero Xpulse 200 4V is a adventure bike. It has 199.0cc engine, gives 40 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.46 Lakh. The Hero Xpulse 200 4V was launched in 2021 as a more capable version of the original Xpulse. It features a 199.6cc oil-cooled engine with a 4-valve head for better highway speeds. The bike is famous for its long-travel suspension and 220mm of ground clearance. It uses a 21-inch front and 18-inch rear spoke wheel setup for off-road durability. An "off-road" variant is also available with fully adjustable suspension and a taller seat. The LED headlamp has been updated for better throw and spread during night rides. It features an integrated luggage rack and a high-mounted exhaust for water wading. The Xpulse is widely considered the most affordable and accessible adventure bike in India.

name: Hero Splendor Plus
text: Hero Splendor Plus is a commuter bike. It has 97.0cc engine, gives 70 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.75 Lakh. The Hero Splendor Plus is a successor to the legendary CD100 launched in 1994. It is powered by a 97.2cc air-cooled engine that has become synonymous with reliability. The bike features Hero's i3S technology which saves fuel by shutting the engine at signals. It has a lightweight tubular double-cradle frame that makes it easy to maneuver. The design is iconic and functional, with a focus on ease of repair and part availability. It offers a comfortable flat seat and a convenient luggage carrier at the rear. The Splendor Plus features "Xtec" variants with digital consoles and Bluetooth connectivity. Millions of Indian households trust this motorcycle for its incredible fuel efficiency and durability.

name: Hero Glamour Xtec
text: Hero Glamour Xtec is a commuter bike. It has 124.7cc engine, gives 60 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.88 Lakh. Hero launched the Glamour Xtec in 2021 as a high-tech version of its popular 125cc bike. It features a 124.7cc engine that produces 10.7 bhp and 10.6 Nm of torque. The Xtec variant brings a fully digital instrument cluster with Bluetooth connectivity. It offers first-in-segment features like turn-by-turn navigation and an integrated USB charger. The bike is equipped with an LED headlamp and a sporty H-signature LED tail lamp. It uses Heroâ€™s revolutionary i3S (Idle Stop-Start System) for enhanced fuel savings in traffic. The riding posture is upright and comfortable, suitable for long hours of city commuting. It remains a popular choice for those who want a premium-looking 125cc bike with modern tech.

name: Bajaj Pulsar N250
text: Bajaj Pulsar N250 is a sports bike. It has 249.0cc engine, gives 35 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.51 Lakh. Bajaj launched the Pulsar N250 in October 2021 to mark the Pulsar brand's 20th anniversary. It is powered by a 249cc oil-cooled engine that delivers 24.5 PS of peak power. The 2024 update added 37mm upside-down (USD) front forks for improved ride quality. It now features a fully digital LCD console with Bluetooth connectivity for navigation. The bike includes a traction control system and three levels of ABS modes for safety. Its design includes a bi-functional LED projector headlamp with sleek "wolf-eyed" DRLs. The engine is paired with a 5-speed gearbox featuring an assist and slipper clutch. It serves as a reliable and powerful all-rounder for both urban and highway use.

name: Bajaj Dominar 400
text: Bajaj Dominar 400 is a sports touring bike. It has 373.0cc engine, gives 28 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 2.5 Lakh. The Bajaj Dominar 400 first debuted in 2016 as a high-performance power cruiser. It uses a 373.3cc DOHC liquid-cooled engine producing 40 PS and 35 Nm. In 2021, the model was updated with factory-fitted touring accessories like a tall visor. The bike features a twin-barrel exhaust that provides a deep, throaty exhaust note. It is built on a perimeter frame and features 43mm USD front forks for stability. The braking system includes a large 320mm front disc with dual-channel ABS. It includes a unique split digital console with a secondary display on the fuel tank. It has a strong reputation as a robust long-distance tourer for diverse Indian terrains.

name: Bajaj Pulsar NS200
text: Bajaj Pulsar NS200 is a sports bike. It has 199.0cc engine, gives 35 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.58 Lakh. The Pulsar NS200 was launched in 2012 and introduced liquid-cooling to the Pulsar range. It features a 199.5cc triple-spark engine that produces 24.5 PS of power. The bike is built on a high-rigidity perimeter frame for exceptional cornering ability. In 2023, Bajaj updated the NS200 with upside-down forks and dual-channel ABS. Its aggressive streetfighter styling has made it a favorite among college students for years. The underbelly exhaust helps in mass centralization for better handling and balance. It features a semi-digital instrument cluster that includes a shift light and gear indicator. This motorcycle remains a top choice for those seeking raw power and sporty handling.

name: Bajaj Avenger Cruise 220
text: Bajaj Avenger Cruise 220 is a cruiser bike. It has 220.0cc engine, gives 40 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.44 Lakh. The Bajaj Avenger was first launched in 2005 and has evolved into the Cruise 220 model. It is powered by a 220cc oil-cooled DTS-i engine that is tuned for highway cruising. The bike features a classic cruiser design with heavy chrome finishes and a tall windshield. It offers a low-slung seat and forward-set footpegs for a very relaxed riding posture. The "Cruise" variant comes with spoke wheels and a padded backrest for the pillion. Safety is provided by a large front disc brake with a single-channel ABS system. It features a digital instrument cluster that blends modern tech with retro cruiser styling. This bike is ideal for riders who want an affordable classic cruiser for long road trips.

name: Bajaj Pulsar 125
text: Bajaj Pulsar 125 is a commuter bike. It has 124.0cc engine, gives 52 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.85 Lakh. Bajaj launched the Pulsar 125 in 2019 to make the Pulsar brand more accessible. It uses the same chassis and bodywork as the Pulsar 150 but with a 125cc engine. The 124.4cc DTS-i engine produces a healthy 11.8 PS and 10.8 Nm of torque. It features a 5-speed gearbox and a front disc brake as standard in most variants. The bike offers a "split seat" version which gives it a much sportier look than rivals. It comes with the signature "wolf-eyed" halogen headlamp and twin LED tail lamps. The Pulsar 125 is perfect for young riders who want the Pulsar image with better economy. It has quickly become one of the top-selling models in Bajaj's extensive lineup.

name: Suzuki V-Strom SX
text: Suzuki V-Strom SX is a touring bike. It has 249.0cc engine, gives 36 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 2.11 Lakh. Suzuki India launched the V-Strom SX, a 250cc adventure tourer, in April 2022. It is based on the Gixxer 250 platform and shares its oil-cooled engine producing 26.5 PS. The design is inspired by Suzuki's larger V-Strom series, featuring a prominent "beak". It offers a generous ground clearance of 205mm for light off-road trails. The bike rides on a 19-inch front and 17-inch rear wheel setup for versatility. It comes with a digital instrument cluster that supports Bluetooth and call alerts. For practicality, it includes a standard rear luggage rack and a USB charging port. It is positioned as an accessible entry-level ADV for riders who want a "master of all" bike.

name: Suzuki Gixxer SF 250
text: Suzuki Gixxer SF 250 is a sports bike. It has 249.0cc engine, gives 32 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.92 Lakh. The Gixxer SF 250 was launched in 2019 as Suzukiâ€™s flagship "Made in India" sports bike. It features a unique Suzuki Oil Cooling System (SOCS) that keeps the engine lightweight. The 249cc engine produces 26.5 PS and is known for its linear power delivery. It features a fully-faired design that is aerodynamic and visually striking. The bike comes with a dedicated clip-on handlebar for a sporty yet comfortable posture. It is equipped with dual-channel ABS and bright LED headlights for night visibility. The digital instrument cluster includes a gear position indicator and a shift light. This motorcycle is highly praised for its refinement and high-speed stability on highways.

name: Suzuki Hayabusa
text: Suzuki Hayabusa is a superbike bike. It has 1340.0cc engine, gives 17 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 16.9 Lakh. The legendary Suzuki Hayabusa was first launched globally in 1999 and arrived in India later. The third-generation model, launched in 2021, is currently sold as a CKD unit in India. It is powered by a massive 1340cc inline-four engine producing 190 PS of power. The bike features the Suzuki Intelligent Ride System (S.I.R.S) with multiple electronic aids. It includes a 6-axis IMU, traction control, launch control, and cruise control as standard. The iconic aerodynamic shape has been refined for better stability at extremely high speeds. It features an analog-digital instrument cluster that retains the classic four-dial layout. The Hayabusa remains a dream bike for Indian enthusiasts due to its incredible legacy and speed.

name: Suzuki Gixxer 155
text: Suzuki Gixxer 155 is a sports bike. It has 155.0cc engine, gives 45 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.34 Lakh. The Suzuki Gixxer 155 was launched in 2014 and immediately won several "Bike of the Year" awards. It features a 155cc engine equipped with Suzuki Eco Performance (SEP) technology. The bike is known for its exceptional mid-range torque and light, agile handling. It features a muscular design with a stubby twin-exit exhaust for a sporty note. In 2019, it received a major facelift with a new LED headlamp and digital console. The bike is equipped with a single-channel ABS and a wide 140-section rear tire. It offers an upright yet slightly aggressive riding position perfect for city slicing. The Gixxer 155 remains one of the most refined and fun-to-ride bikes in its segment.

name: Yamaha R15 V4
text: Yamaha R15 V4 is a sports bike. It has 155.0cc engine, gives 45 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.82 Lakh. The Yamaha R15 V4 made its Indian debut in 2021, continuing a legendary sportbike series. It is powered by a high-tech 155cc engine featuring Variable Valve Actuation (VVA). The engine produces 18.4 PS and is known for its high-revving nature and refinement. It was the first in its segment to offer a Traction Control System and Quickshifter. The bike features premium hardware like USD front forks and a Deltabox frame for sharp handling. Its design is heavily inspired by the larger Yamaha R7 supersport motorcycle. It includes a digital instrument cluster with "Street" and "Track" display modes. The R15 V4 remains the benchmark for entry-level supersport performance in India.

name: Yamaha MT-15 V2
text: Yamaha MT-15 V2 is a sports bike. It has 155.0cc engine, gives 48 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.68 Lakh. Yamaha launched the MT-15 in India in 2019 as the "Dark Warrior" streetfighter. The V2 update in 2022 added upside-down forks and an aluminum swingarm for better performance. It shares the same 155cc VVA engine with the R15, offering punchy acceleration. The bike features a radical "transformer" style LED headlamp that is very distinctive. It offers a very upright and commanding riding position, perfect for quick city maneuvers. The MT-15 is known for its light weight and "flickability" in heavy traffic conditions. Recent updates have added dual-channel ABS and LED turn indicators as standard. It is a top choice for young riders who want a stylish and high-tech street naked bike.

name: Yamaha FZ-S FI V4
text: Yamaha FZ-S FI V4 is a sports bike. It has 149.0cc engine, gives 50 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.29 Lakh. The Yamaha FZ series was first launched in 2008 and revolutionized the 150cc segment. The Version 4 (V4) was launched recently with a new LED headlamp and traction control. It is powered by a 149cc fuel-injected engine that focuses on low-end torque. The bike features a muscular fuel tank and a wide rear tire for a "big bike" feel. It is one of the lightest bikes in its class, making it very easy to handle. Yamaha includes Bluetooth connectivity via the "Y-Connect" app for phone notifications. The seat is a single-piece unit designed for the comfort of both rider and pillion. It remains a favorite for city commuters who want a muscular look with great fuel economy.

name: Yamaha FZ-X
text: Yamaha FZ-X is a scrambler bike. It has 149.0cc engine, gives 48 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.36 Lakh. Yamaha introduced the FZ-X in 2021 as a neo-retro scrambler based on the FZ platform. It features a round LED headlamp with an oval DRL and a "tuck and roll" seat. The bike is powered by the same reliable 149cc engine found in the FZ-S series. It offers a more upright and relaxed riding position compared to the standard FZ. The bike comes with block-pattern tires that allow for some light trail riding. Safety is enhanced with a single-channel ABS and a standard traction control system. It features a fully digital instrument cluster with smartphone connectivity features. This bike is aimed at riders who want a unique, retro-styled motorcycle for daily use.

name: TVS Sport
text: TVS Sport is a commuter bike. It has 109.7cc engine, gives 70 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.65 Lakh. The TVS Sport was launched in India in 2004 as a fuel‑efficient commuter motorcycle. It is powered by a 109.7cc air‑cooled single‑cylinder engine designed for reliability and low running costs. The engine produces smooth and predictable power delivery that is ideal for city riding. Its lightweight construction helps improve acceleration at low speeds. The motorcycle is tuned to perform efficiently in stop‑and‑go traffic conditions. Engine vibrations are minimal at commuting speeds, improving rider comfort. The gearing is optimized for fuel savings rather than outright speed. Overall, the TVS Sport is engineered for economical daily transportation with dependable performance.

name: TVS Radeon
text: TVS Radeon is a commuter bike. It has 109.7cc engine, gives 65 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.72 Lakh. The TVS Radeon was launched in 2018 targeting the premium commuter segment. It uses a 109.7cc air‑cooled engine focused on refinement and durability. Power delivery is linear and calm, making it easy to ride for new and experienced riders alike. The engine performs best in low‑to‑mid RPM ranges where daily commuting happens. It offers good torque response for carrying a pillion or light luggage. Heat management is well controlled for urban environments. The suspension setup complements the engine for a comfortable ride. Overall, the Radeon prioritizes comfort, reliability, and steady performance.

name: Honda SP 125
text: Honda SP 125 is a commuter bike. It has 124.0cc engine, gives 60 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.85 Lakh. The Honda SP 125 was introduced in 2019 as a modern 125cc commuter motorcycle. It is powered by a 124cc air‑cooled engine with fuel injection for improved efficiency. The engine delivers smooth throttle response and consistent power output. Low engine noise and vibration levels enhance long‑term riding comfort. The motor performs efficiently during city rides and moderate highway use. Torque delivery is tuned for quick overtakes in traffic. Honda’s engine reliability ensures long service life. Overall, the SP 125 balances performance, refinement, and fuel economy.

name: Honda Unicorn
text: Honda Unicorn is a commuter bike. It has 162.7cc engine, gives 50 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.1 Lakh. The Honda Unicorn was launched in 2004 and remains a cult favorite in the 150-160cc segment. It is powered by a 162.7cc engine that is famous for its "butter-smooth" refinement. The bike was the first in India to introduce a mono-shock rear suspension system. Its design has remained largely unchanged over two decades, emphasizing a timeless, sober look. It features an upright riding posture that ensures zero fatigue during long city rides. The Unicorn comes with a long, well-padded seat that is comfortable for both rider and pillion. It is equipped with a single-channel ABS and a front disc brake for reliable stopping power. This motorcycle is highly regarded for its bulletproof reliability and consistent fuel efficiency. Overall, the Unicorn is engineered for comfort‑oriented everyday performance.

name: Hero HF 100
text: Hero HF 100 is a commuter bike. It has 97.2cc engine, gives 70 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.55 Lakh. The Hero HF 100 was launched in 2020 as an entry‑level commuter motorcycle. It is powered by a 97.2cc air‑cooled engine optimized for fuel efficiency. The engine delivers gentle and smooth acceleration for city usage. Mechanical simplicity ensures low maintenance requirements. The power output is tuned for consistent performance rather than speed. Heat dissipation is effective for daily riding conditions. The bike performs reliably even under heavy daily usage. Overall, the HF 100 focuses on economical and dependable performance.

name: Bajaj Platina 100
text: Bajaj Platina 100 is a commuter bike. It has 102.0cc engine, gives 70 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.68 Lakh. The Bajaj Platina 100 was launched in 2006 focusing on rider comfort and efficiency. It uses a 102cc air‑cooled engine tuned for smooth power delivery. The engine performs efficiently at low RPMs, ideal for city traffic. Torque delivery is gentle and predictable for relaxed riding. The motorcycle maintains stability at consistent cruising speeds. Low vibration levels enhance comfort over long distances. The engine is engineered for longevity and easy maintenance. Overall, the Platina 100 emphasizes comfort‑oriented daily performance.

name: TVS Ronin TD
text: TVS Ronin TD is a scrambler bike. It has 225.0cc engine, gives 42 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.75 Lakh. The TVS Ronin TD is a premium "modern-retro" motorcycle that was first introduced in 2022 to carve out a new lifestyle segment. It is powered by a 225.9cc oil-cooled engine that generates a maximum power of 20.4 PS and a peak torque of 19.93 Nm. The bike is capable of reaching a top speed of approximately 120 kmph, making it suitable for both city commutes and relaxed highway cruising. Its unique square-stroke engine design ensures a very strong low-end and mid-range performance, which is ideal for navigating urban traffic. The TD variant stands out with its triple-tone color schemes and gold-finished upside-down (USD) front forks. It features a signature T-shaped LED DRL and a fully digital offset instrument cluster that adds to its neo-retro appeal. The motorcycle is equipped with dual-channel ABS that offers specific modes for Urban and Rain to ensure safety on various surfaces. It also includes SmartXonnect technology, providing voice assistance and turn-by-turn navigation directly on the console. The Ronin TD is designed for riders who value a versatile, unscripted riding experience with high-end features.

name: Honda CB350RS
text: Honda CB350RS is a scrambler bike. It has 348.0cc engine, gives 35 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 2.15 Lakh. The Honda CB350RS was launched in 2021 as a sportier, scrambler-inspired version of the H'ness CB350. It utilizes a refined 348.36cc air-cooled engine that delivers a maximum power of 21.07 PS and a peak torque of 30 Nm. The motorcycle can achieve a top speed of 125 kmph, providing a smooth and effortless performance on open highways. It features a more aggressive riding posture compared to its sibling, with rear-set footpegs and a tucked-in handlebar. The design is characterized by its wide, block-pattern tires and a sleek "tuck and roll" seat for a rugged aesthetic. It comes equipped with Honda Selectable Torque Control (HSTC) to manage rear-wheel traction on slippery roads. The bike features a full LED lighting system and a compact semi-digital instrument cluster with Bluetooth connectivity. Its assist and slipper clutch makes gear changes light and prevents rear-wheel locking during aggressive downshifts. The CB350RS is a perfect blend of retro charm and modern engineering for enthusiasts who enjoy weekend explorations.

name: Hero Xtreme 200S 4V
text: Hero Xtreme 200S 4V is a sports bike. It has 199.0cc engine, gives 40 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.41 Lakh. Hero MotoCorp introduced the Xtreme 200S 4V in 2023 to provide a fully-faired, sporty option for the 200cc segment. It is powered by a 199.6cc oil-cooled 4-valve engine that produces a peak power of 19.1 PS and a maximum torque of 17.35 Nm. This motorcycle can reach a top speed of 115 kmph, offering a balance of aerodynamic efficiency and stable performance. The 4-valve head significantly improves the engine's high-speed refinement and top-end power compared to the older model. It features a striking full-fairing design with an integrated LED headlamp that provides excellent visibility at night. The digital instrument cluster comes with Bluetooth connectivity, offering turn-by-turn navigation and call alerts for the rider. It uses a 7-step adjustable mono-shock at the rear to ensure a comfortable ride across different road conditions. The bike is equipped with single-channel ABS and petal disc brakes to ensure reliable stopping power in emergency situations. It remains a popular choice for young riders who want a big-bike look with manageable daily running costs.

name: Bajaj Avenger Street 160
text: Bajaj Avenger Street 160 is a cruiser bike. It has 160.0cc engine, gives 45 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.18 Lakh. The Bajaj Avenger Street 160 was launched in 2019 to replace the older 150cc version and provide an entry-level cruiser experience. It features a 160cc air-cooled, fuel-injected engine that produces 15 PS of power and 13.7 Nm of peak torque. The motorcycle has a top speed of approximately 105 kmph, making it ideal for relaxed urban cruising and short highway stints. Its low-slung seat and forward-set footpegs provide an extremely comfortable and laid-back riding posture for the rider. The "Street" aesthetic is defined by its blacked-out components, alloy wheels, and a smaller, sporty handlebar. It is equipped with a single-channel ABS system to ensure safety during sudden braking on city roads. The bike features a classic semi-digital instrument cluster that fits perfectly into its modern cruiser styling. It uses a telescopic front fork and twin shock absorbers at the rear to absorb road imperfections effectively. The Avenger Street 160 is highly regarded as the most affordable cruiser in the Indian market for daily commuters.

name: Suzuki Gixxer 250
text: Suzuki Gixxer 250 is a sports bike. It has 249.0cc engine, gives 35 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.85 Lakh. The Suzuki Gixxer 250 made its Indian debut in 2019 as a premium naked streetfighter with Japanese engineering at its core. It is powered by a 249cc oil-cooled, fuel-injected engine that generates 26.5 PS of power and 22.2 Nm of torque. This motorcycle is capable of reaching a top speed of 150 kmph, offering high-speed stability and a refined engine character. The Suzuki Oil Cooling System (SOCS) ensures that the engine remains lightweight and thermally efficient during spirited rides. Its design is muscular and aggressive, featuring a sharp LED headlamp and a stubby twin-exit exhaust for a sporty note. It comes equipped with a dual-channel ABS system as standard to provide maximum braking confidence on all surfaces. The fully digital instrument cluster offers a gear position indicator and a shift light to help the rider manage power delivery. The bike features a wide 140-section rear tire that provides excellent grip and stability during sharp cornering. It is a top choice for riders moving up to the quarter-liter segment who value performance and build quality.

name: Yamaha FZ-FI
text: Yamaha FZ-FI  is a sports bike. It has 149.0cc engine, gives 50 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.17 Lakh. The Yamaha FZ-FI  was launched in 2019, continuing the legacy of the original FZ series that revolutionized the 150cc segment. It features a reliable 149cc fuel-injected engine that produces 12.4 PS of power and 13.3 Nm of peak torque. The motorcycle can reach a top speed of about 115 kmph, focusing on a strong mid-range and excellent fuel economy for city use. Its design is characterized by a massive, muscular fuel tank and a compact LED headlamp that gives it a "street-naked" stance. The bike is one of the lightest in its category, which makes it incredibly agile and easy to handle in heavy urban traffic. It comes with a single-channel ABS and a wide 140-section radial rear tire to ensure safety and stability. The single-piece seat is designed for maximum comfort for both the rider and a pillion passenger during daily commutes. It features a negative LCD instrument cluster that is clear and easy to read even in bright sunlight. The FZ-FI V3 remains a favorite for office commuters who want a stylish bike that is very light on maintenance.

name: Honda CB300F
text: Honda CB300F is a street bike. It has 293.0cc engine, gives 35 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.7 Lakh. The Honda CB300F is a modern streetfighter designed for aggressive urban riding and weekend highway sprints. It is powered by a 293.52cc oil-cooled engine that delivers a peak power of 24.5 PS and a maximum torque of 25.6 Nm. The motorcycle is capable of reaching a top speed of 150 kmph, making it a competitive player in the sub-500cc naked segment. Its design is characterized by sharp, muscular tank shrouds and a compact LED headlamp that gives it a "Big Bike" feel. The bike features a 6-speed gearbox with an assist and slipper clutch to ensure smooth and effortless gear transitions during rapid downshifting. For safety, it is equipped with dual-channel ABS and a traction control system, which is a rare feature in this price bracket. The fully digital instrument cluster offers five levels of brightness adjustment and includes a gear position indicator. With its wide 150-section rear tire and USD front forks, the CB300F provides exceptional stability and handling through corners. It is the perfect choice for riders seeking Japanese reliability paired with a sporty, naked aesthetic.

name: Hero Xtreme 125R
text: Hero Xtreme 125R is a sports bike. It has 125.0cc engine, gives 66 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.95 Lakh. The Hero Xtreme 125R was launched in 2024 to disrupt the premium 125cc segment with its radical, sporty styling. It is equipped with a 124.7cc Sprint-EBT engine that produces 11.4 PS of power and a peak torque of 10.5 Nm. Despite its smaller displacement, the bike can reach a respectable top speed of 105 kmph, which is impressive for its class. The motorcycle features a first-in-segment 37mm thick front telescopic fork and a 7-step adjustable monoshock by Showa. It comes with a wide 120-section rear tire that provides a planted feel usually found on 150cc motorcycles. The all-LED lighting setup, including the projector headlamp and DRLs, gives it a very futuristic and aggressive appearance. Hero has integrated a single-channel ABS system to provide superior braking confidence to young riders and commuters. The fuel efficiency is a major highlight, as the bike is engineered to deliver a class-leading mileage of 66 kmpl. It is an ideal entry-level machine for those who want the looks of a streetfighter with the economy of a commuter.

name: Bajaj Pulsar N160
text: Bajaj Pulsar N160 is a sports bike. It has 165.0cc engine, gives 55 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.32 Lakh. The Bajaj Pulsar N160 is a refined street motorcycle built on the new-generation Pulsar platform introduced in 2022. It features a 164.82cc oil-cooled engine that generates 16 PS of power and a maximum torque of 14.65 Nm. The bike is capable of achieving a top speed of 120 kmph, offering a very linear and smooth power delivery across the rev range. One of its standout features is the dual-channel ABS, making it one of the safest motorcycles in the 160cc category. The design is inspired by the larger N250, featuring a signature LED projector headlamp with brow-shaped DRLs. Its underbelly exhaust helps in better mass centralization, which significantly improves the bike's flickability in city traffic. The gear-shift feel is incredibly crisp, thanks to the constant-mesh 5-speed transmission that handles urban commutes with ease. It also features a USB charging port conveniently located near the fuel tank for charging mobile devices on the go. The Pulsar N160 is highly praised for its engine refinement and premium build quality compared to previous Pulsar generations.

name: Bajaj Pulsar NS400Z
text: Bajaj Pulsar NS400Z is a hyper-naked bike. It has 373.0cc engine, gives 30 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.85 Lakh. The Bajaj Pulsar NS400Z is the most powerful Pulsar ever made, launched in 2024 to dominate the sub-400cc performance segment. It shares its 373.3cc liquid-cooled DOHC engine with the Dominar 400, producing a massive 40 PS of power and 35 Nm of peak torque. This hyper-naked motorcycle can reach a top speed of 155 kmph, offering blistering acceleration that can challenge bikes twice its price. It features an advanced electronics suite including four riding modes (Road, Rain, Sport, Off-road) and a switchable traction control system. The design is an evolution of the NS series, featuring a more aggressive lightning-bolt DRL and a muscular fuel tank. It is equipped with premium 43mm gold-finished USD forks and a large 320mm front disc brake for exceptional control. The bike also features a full-color LCD console with Bluetooth connectivity for navigation and music control. With its competitive pricing, the NS400Z offers an unparalleled power-to-money ratio for performance enthusiasts in India. It is the ultimate upgrade for existing Pulsar fans looking for a big-bore streetfighter experience.

name: TVS Apache RTR 310
text: TVS Apache RTR 310 is a sports bike. It has 312.0cc engine, gives 30 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 2.53 Lakh. The TVS Apache RTR 310 is a flagship naked streetfighter designed for aggressive performance and urban agility. It is powered by a 312.2cc liquid-cooled engine that produces a maximum power of 35.6 PS and a peak torque of 28.7 Nm. This engine allows the bike to reach a top speed of 150 kmph with remarkable stability. The motorcycle features a unique reverse-inclined engine layout for better mass centralization. It comes equipped with advanced technology like climate-controlled seats and a bi-directional quickshifter for seamless gear changes. Riders can choose from five distinct riding modes including Urban, Rain, Sports, Track, and Supermoto. The 5-inch TFT cluster offers GoPro control and music sync via SmartXonnect. Its sharp trellis frame and adjustable suspension ensure precise handling on both streets and tracks. The bike also introduces segment-first features like dynamic twin LED headlamps that adjust brightness based on speed. Overall, it represents the pinnacle of TVS engineering in the sub-500cc naked segment.

name: Hero Mavrick 440
text: Hero Mavrick 440 is a cruiser bike. It has 440.0cc engine, gives 32 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.99 Lakh. The Hero Mavrick 440 is the brand's most powerful motorcycle to date, built on a robust 440cc platform. This air-oil cooled single-cylinder engine delivers a maximum power of 27.4 PS and an impressive peak torque of 36 Nm. The bike is capable of achieving a top speed of approximately 140 kmph, making it ideal for highway cruising. Its design language is a blend of a muscular roadster and a classic cruiser, featuring a signature round LED headlamp with H-shaped DRLs. The motorcycle is built on a trellis frame that provides great structural rigidity for stable high-speed runs. It features a digital instrument cluster that offers turn-by-turn navigation and smartphone connectivity. The 6-speed gearbox is paired with an assist and slipper clutch to ensure smooth downshifts. With a comfortable 803mm seat height, it caters well to a wide range of Indian riders. The Mavrick 440 focuses on delivering strong low-end torque for effortless city overtakes and relaxed long-distance touring.

name: Honda CB350
text: Honda CB350 is a cruiser bike. It has 348.0cc engine, gives 35 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 2.0 Lakh. The Honda CB350 is a classic retro-styled motorcycle designed to compete in the popular 350cc cruiser segment in India. It is powered by a 348.36cc air-cooled engine that produces a maximum power of 21.1 PS and a peak torque of 29.4 Nm. The bike can comfortably reach a top speed of 130 kmph, providing a smooth and vibration-free ride. It features a long, metallic fender and a classic round LED headlamp that enhances its vintage appeal. The engine is mated to a 5-speed gearbox with an assist and slipper clutch for easy lever operation. Honda has equipped this model with the Honda Selectable Torque Control (HSTC) to prevent rear-wheel slip. The "Roaring Exhaust Note" is specifically tuned to give it a deep, thumping sound that enthusiasts love. It also features the Honda Smartphone Voice Control system (HSVCS) for hands-free connectivity. This motorcycle offers a perfect blend of old-school charm and modern Japanese reliability.

name: TVS Apache RTR 160 (2V)
text: TVS Apache RTR 160 (2V) is a sports bike. It has 160.0cc engine, gives 45 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.19 Lakh. The TVS Apache RTR 160 (2V) is one of the longest-running performance commuters in India. It features a 159.7cc single-cylinder engine that produces a maximum power of 16.04 PS and a peak torque of 13.85 Nm. The motorcycle can reach a top speed of approximately 107 kmph, making it suitable for quick urban sprints. Its racing-inspired design includes a sharp fuel tank and an LED headlamp for better visibility. The bike comes equipped with a Glide Through Technology (GTT) for low-speed urban crawling. For safety, it uses a single-channel Super Moto ABS system. The semi-digital instrument cluster provides essential ride data and a top speed recorder. It offers a balanced riding posture that is both sporty and comfortable for daily city use. The exhaust note is tuned to provide a throaty, racing sound characteristic of the Apache series. Overall, it remains a reliable and affordable entry into the TVS racing heritage.

name: TVS Apache RTR 180
text: TVS Apache RTR 180 is a sports bike. It has 177.0cc engine, gives 40 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.32 Lakh. The TVS Apache RTR 180 is a legendary streetfighter known for its agility and sharp handling. It is powered by a 177.4cc oil-cooled engine that delivers 17.02 PS of power and 15.5 Nm of peak torque. This allows the bike to achieve a top speed of 113 kmph, catering to performance enthusiasts. The 2022 update brought a significant weight reduction and improved power-to-weight ratio. It features SmartXonnect technology for turn-by-turn navigation and call alerts on the go. The bike offers three riding modes—Urban, Rain, and Sport—to adapt to different weather conditions. Its suspension setup includes telescopic front forks and gas-filled rear shocks for a plush ride. The braking system features petal discs at both ends for superior heat dissipation and bite. An LED headlamp and tail lamp give it a modern and aggressive road presence. It is widely respected as a versatile machine for both daily commutes and weekend highway runs.

name: TVS Star City Plus
text: TVS Star City Plus is a commuter bike. It has 110.0cc engine, gives 70 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.78 Lakh. The TVS Star City Plus is a premium commuter motorcycle designed for maximum fuel efficiency and reliability. It features a refined 109.7cc Eco-Thrust engine that produces 8.19 PS of power and 8.7 Nm of peak torque. The motorcycle can reach a top speed of 90 kmph, which is sufficient for city commuting. Its fuel efficiency is among the best in its class, reaching up to 70 kmpl under ideal conditions. The bike is equipped with a Roto Petal Disc Brake for enhanced stopping power in urban traffic. It features a stylish LED headlamp and a semi-digital console with a service reminder. The seat is long and well-cushioned to provide comfort for both the rider and a pillion. It also comes with a convenient USB charging port to keep devices powered during the commute. The five-step adjustable rear shock absorbers allow for a customized ride based on the load. It is an ideal choice for office-goers and delivery professionals who prioritize low running costs.

name: Honda Shine 100
text: Honda Shine 100 is a commuter bike. It has 99.0cc engine, gives 65 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.65 Lakh. The Honda Shine 100 is an entry-level commuter launched in 2023 to offer legendary reliability at an affordable price. It is powered by a 98.98cc fuel-injected engine that generates 7.38 PS of power and 8.05 Nm of peak torque. This motorcycle is capable of reaching a top speed of 85 kmph, focusing on high mileage and ease of use. It features a long, flat seat that is comfortable for small families and rural users. The bike is equipped with a Combi-Brake System (CBS) with an equalizer for better stability during braking. Its lightweight frame and low seat height make it exceptionally easy to handle for new riders. The engine is equipped with an auto-choke system and a side-stand engine cut-off for added safety. It features a simple analog instrument cluster that is clear and easy to read. The suspension system is tuned to absorb bumps on rough rural roads effectively. Overall, it is designed for those who need a no-nonsense, fuel-efficient daily driver with low maintenance.

name: Honda Livo
text: Honda Livo is a commuter bike. It has 110.0cc engine, gives 60 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.8 Lakh. The Honda Livo is a stylish 110cc commuter that aims to bring premium looks to the executive segment. It is powered by a 109.51cc engine delivering 8.79 PS of power and 9.30 Nm of torque. The motorcycle has a top speed of about 86 kmph and is known for its refined engine performance. It features an integrated engine start/stop switch for better convenience at traffic signals. The bike comes with a digital-analog instrument cluster that includes a service due reminder. Its sharp tank shrouds and aggressive headlamp design distinguish it from typical commuters. The five-step adjustable rear suspension ensures a comfortable ride over various road surfaces. It is equipped with a front disc brake option and CBS for improved safety during emergencies. The tubeless tires and maintenance-free battery contribute to its low ownership costs. It is an excellent option for urban riders who want a balance of style and fuel economy.

name: Honda SP160
text: Honda SP160 is a sports bike. It has 163.0cc engine, gives 45 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.18 Lakh. The Honda SP160 is a premium commuter motorcycle that shares its core with the legendary Unicorn but offers modern styling. It features a refined 162.71cc engine that produces 13.46 PS of power and 14.58 Nm of peak torque. The motorcycle can reach a top speed of 115 kmph, providing a smooth and effortless performance on highways. It comes with a fully digital instrument cluster that displays a gear position indicator and clock. The bike features a signature LED headlamp and a bold LED tail lamp for a premium look. It is equipped with a mono-shock rear suspension that offers superior stability and comfort. The braking duties are handled by petal disc brakes at the front and rear, assisted by single-channel ABS. Its wide 130mm rear tire ensures excellent grip and road presence during high-speed runs. The engine is equipped with a counter-balancer to minimize vibrations, ensuring a smooth ride. It is designed for those who want a practical commuter with a touch of sporty aesthetics.

name: Hero HF Deluxe
text: Hero HF Deluxe is a commuter bike. It has 97.0cc engine, gives 70 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.68 Lakh. The Hero HF Deluxe is one of the most trusted and highest-selling entry-level motorcycles in India. It is powered by the legendary 97.2cc air-cooled engine that delivers 8.02 PS of power and 8.05 Nm of torque. The motorcycle has a top speed of 87 kmph and is revered for its bulletproof reliability. It features Hero's i3S technology (Idle Stop-Start System) to save fuel during long waits at traffic lights. The bike is equipped with a side-stand engine cut-off and integrated braking system for safety. Its simple and functional design makes it easy to maintain even in remote rural areas. The seat is long and flat, providing ample space for two adults and cargo. It features a maintenance-free battery and tubeless tires for a hassle-free ownership experience. The suspension system is built to handle the rough and unpredictable terrains of rural India. It remains a top choice for those seeking a durable, low-cost commuter with excellent resale value.

name: Hero Passion Plus
text: Hero Passion Plus is a commuter bike. It has 97.0cc engine, gives 65 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.78 Lakh. The Hero Passion Plus was re-introduced in 2023 to cater to fans of the classic, stylish commuter. It utilizes the reliable 97.2cc engine producing 8.02 PS of power and 8.05 Nm of peak torque. The bike can reach a top speed of 87 kmph, focusing on a comfortable and efficient urban riding experience. It features a stylish cowl and graphics that give it a more premium look than the HF series. The motorcycle comes with an electric start and an i3S system for improved fuel economy. It includes a semi-digital instrument cluster and a handy USB charging port for mobile devices. The upright riding posture and well-padded seat make it ideal for long daily commutes. It is equipped with a combined braking system (CBS) to ensure safe and balanced stops. The bike also features a side-stand indicator to remind the rider for safety before starting. It is a perfect choice for middle-class Indian riders who want a bit more style from their 100cc bike.

name: Hero Xpulse 200T 4V
text: Hero Xpulse 200T 4V is a touring bike. It has 200.0cc engine, gives 40 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.4 Lakh. The Hero Xpulse 200T 4V is the road-focused, touring-oriented version of the popular Xpulse adventure bike. It is powered by a 199.6cc 4-valve oil-cooled engine that produces 19.1 PS of power and 17.3 Nm of torque. This allowing the bike to achieve a top speed of 115 kmph, providing better highway performance than its 2V predecessor. The motorcycle features a neo-retro design with a round LED headlamp and stylish graphics. It comes with a digital instrument cluster that offers turn-by-turn navigation and Bluetooth connectivity. The seat is lowered and the tires are switched to 17-inch alloys for better on-road agility and stability. It features a rear luggage rack which is very convenient for mounting touring gear or a top box. For safety, it is equipped with single-channel ABS and disc brakes at both the front and rear. The upright riding posture ensures comfort during long-distance rides on paved highways. It is an excellent budget touring option for riders who prefer the road over the dirt.

name: Hero Splendor + XTEC
text: Hero Splendor + XTEC is a commuter bike. It has 97.0cc engine, gives 70 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.8 Lakh. The Hero Splendor + XTEC is a modernized version of India's most iconic commuter, the Splendor Plus. It is powered by the time-tested 97.2cc engine that produces 8.02 PS of power and 8.05 Nm of peak torque. The motorcycle can reach a top speed of 87 kmph while maintaining an incredible fuel economy of up to 70 kmpl. The XTEC variant introduces a full-digital instrument cluster with Bluetooth connectivity for call and SMS notifications. It also features a distinctive LED High Intensity Position Lamp (HIPL) and a USB charging port. The i3S technology continues to provide fuel savings by shutting off the engine when idle in traffic. Safety is prioritized with a bank-angle sensor that shuts off the engine in case of a fall. The bike retains its classic rectangular headlamp but with refreshed graphics and a new color palette. It features a side-stand engine cut-off and a combined braking system for enhanced rider safety. It is the ultimate choice for those who want the legendary Splendor reliability with modern connectivity features.

name: Bajaj Pulsar 150
text: Bajaj Pulsar 150 is a sports bike. It has 150.0cc engine, gives 50 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.15 Lakh. The Bajaj Pulsar 150 is a definitive sports commuter that helped define the performance segment in India. It is powered by a 149.5cc DTS-i engine that produces 14 PS of power and 13.25 Nm of peak torque. This allows the bike to reach a top speed of 115 kmph, providing a punchy and exciting ride in city traffic. It features the iconic 'wolf-eyed' headlamp and a muscular fuel tank that give it an aggressive look. The bike is equipped with a 5-speed gearbox that offers smooth and precise shifting. It comes with a front disc brake and single-channel ABS to ensure reliable stopping power. The semi-digital instrument cluster provides essential information like speed, fuel level, and service indicators. Its twin-shock rear suspension and telescopic front forks provide a balanced and comfortable ride quality. The wide tires and long wheelbase ensure stability during high-speed highway cruising. It remains a top seller due to its perfect balance of performance, style, and everyday usability.

name: Bajaj Pulsar 220F
text: Bajaj Pulsar 220F is a sports bike. It has 220.0cc engine, gives 38 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.41 Lakh. The Bajaj Pulsar 220F is one of India's most beloved performance motorcycles, often referred to as the 'Fastest Indian' at its peak. It features a 220cc oil-cooled fuel-injected engine that delivers 20.4 PS of power and 18.55 Nm of peak torque. The bike can achieve a top speed of 134 kmph, making it a favorite for highway touring and enthusiastic riding. It is easily recognized by its semi-fairing design and projector headlamp, which provides excellent night visibility. The motorcycle comes with a 5-speed gearbox and a heavy-duty clutch to handle the engine's power delivery. It is equipped with disc brakes at both ends and single-channel ABS for enhanced rider safety. The semi-digital console remains a classic, offering a large tachometer and digital speedometer. Clip-on handlebars and a split-seat setup provide a sporty yet comfortable riding posture. Its large 15-liter fuel tank ensures a decent range for long-distance highway journeys. Despite its age, it continues to enjoy a cult following due to its raw performance and affordable price.

name: Bajaj Pulsar RS200
text: Bajaj Pulsar RS200 is a sports bike. It has 200.0cc engine, gives 35 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.76 Lakh. The Bajaj Pulsar RS200 is the flagship fully-faired motorcycle in the Pulsar lineup, designed for high-speed performance. It is powered by a 199.5cc liquid-cooled, fuel-injected engine that produces a significant 24.5 PS and 18.7 Nm of torque. The motorcycle can reach a top speed of 140 kmph, offering a thrilling experience on both open roads and race tracks. It features an aggressive aerodynamic design with dual projector headlamps and a unique LED tail lamp. The bike comes equipped with a 6-speed gearbox to optimize its high-revving engine's power delivery. It features a dual-channel ABS system that provides exceptional braking confidence and safety in all conditions. The perimeter frame design ensures the bike remains stable and flickable during tight cornering. It also includes a digital-analog instrument cluster that is packed with features for the performance rider. The suspension setup includes telescopic front forks and a Nitrox mono-shock at the rear for a superior ride. It is an ideal choice for young riders seeking a mini-supersport experience at an accessible price.

name: Bajaj Pulsar NS160
text: Bajaj Pulsar NS160 is a sports bike. It has 160.0cc engine, gives 45 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.47 Lakh. The Bajaj Pulsar NS160 is a powerful naked streetfighter designed for riders who prioritize handling and high-revving performance. It features a 160.3cc oil-cooled engine that delivers 17.2 PS of power and 14.6 Nm of peak torque. This allows the motorcycle to reach a top speed of 120 kmph, making it one of the most powerful in its class. The bike is built on a high-rigidity perimeter frame that provides exceptional stability during high-speed cornering. For the latest update, it was equipped with upside-down (USD) front forks and dual-channel ABS for superior control. It features an aggressive streetfighter stance with a muscular tank and a sleek underbelly exhaust. The instrument cluster is a digital-analog unit that includes a gear position indicator and real-time fuel efficiency. Its 5-speed gearbox is optimized to deliver punchy performance across the rev range. The split-seat and clip-on handlebars provide an engaging and sporty riding posture. It is a favorite among young performance-oriented riders looking for a naked sportbike for the city.

name: Bajaj Pulsar NS125
text: Bajaj Pulsar NS125 is a sports bike. It has 125.0cc engine, gives 55 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.05 Lakh. The Bajaj Pulsar NS125 is the entry-level model in the high-performance NS (Naked Sport) series. It is powered by a 124.45cc engine that produces 12 PS of power and 11 Nm of peak torque. The motorcycle can reach a top speed of 103 kmph, offering a much more exciting ride than typical 125cc commuters. It inherits the aggressive streetfighter design of its larger siblings, including the perimeter frame and muscular bodywork. The bike is equipped with a 5-speed gearbox and a Nitrox mono-shock rear suspension for a sporty ride. It features a 240mm front disc brake with a combined braking system (CBS) for improved stopping performance. The semi-digital instrument cluster provides clear and concise ride data in an easy-to-read format. Its lightweight design and sharp handling make it a joy to ride in congested urban environments. The split-seat and wolf-eyed headlamp contribute to its premium and sporty appeal for young buyers. It is an ideal choice for college students and first-time buyers who want a sportbike feel with low running costs.

name: Bajaj CT 110X
text: Bajaj CT 110X is a commuter bike. It has 115.0cc engine, gives 70 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.7 Lakh. The Bajaj CT 110X is a rugged and durable commuter designed specifically for tough rural roads and heavy-duty usage. It is powered by a 115.45cc engine that delivers 8.6 PS of power and 9.81 Nm of peak torque. The motorcycle can reach a top speed of 90 kmph and is renowned for its exceptional fuel efficiency. It features a robust design with a circular headlamp protected by a grille and a large rear luggage rack. The bike is equipped with semi-knobby tires that provide excellent grip on unpaved and slushy roads. Its high ground clearance of 170mm ensures it can handle deep potholes and bumps with ease. The crash guards and tank pads add a layer of protection and comfort for the rider during long hours of work. It comes with a simple analog console that is easy to read and extremely durable in harsh weather. The SNS (Spring in Spring) suspension system provides a plush ride even with heavy loads. It is the ultimate workhorse for delivery professionals and riders in challenging terrains.

name: Bajaj Platina 110
text: Bajaj Platina 110 is a commuter bike. It has 115.0cc engine, gives 70 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.73 Lakh. The Bajaj Platina 110 is a premium commuter designed to offer maximum comfort and safety to the rider. It is powered by a 115.45cc engine that delivers 8.6 PS of power and 9.81 Nm of peak torque. The motorcycle can reach a top speed of 90 kmph and is famous for its incredible fuel economy and smooth ride. It is the first and only bike in its class to offer a single-channel ABS for superior braking safety. The 'ComforTec' technology includes a long-travel suspension and a quilted seat for a vibration-free ride. It features an LED DRL and a digital instrument cluster that shows a gear position indicator. The bike is equipped with a 5-speed gearbox (H-Gear) which allows for relaxed cruising on the highway. Its wide footrests and large grab rail provide extra comfort for the pillion passenger. The tubeless tires and maintenance-free battery further reduce the overall ownership and service costs. It is an ideal machine for long-distance daily commuters who prioritize safety and comfort above all else.

name: Bajaj Dominar 250
text: Bajaj Dominar 250 is a sports bike. It has 249.0cc engine, gives 35 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.85 Lakh. The Bajaj Dominar 250 is the 'Sports Touring' entry in the Dominar family, designed for long-distance touring on a budget. It is powered by a 248.8cc liquid-cooled engine derived from the KTM 250 Duke, producing 27 PS and 23.5 Nm of torque. This allows the motorcycle to reach a top speed of 132 kmph, providing a stable and comfortable performance on highways. It features high-quality USD front forks and a Nitrox mono-shock for excellent handling and ride quality. The bike is equipped with a dual-channel ABS system and large disc brakes to ensure maximum safety. Its full-LED lighting setup including the headlamp and tail lamp provide premium aesthetics and great visibility. The twin-barrel exhaust provides a deep and bassy engine note that is characteristic of the Dominar range. It features a secondary display on the fuel tank for additional ride information like the clock and gear position. The slipper clutch ensures smooth downshifts during high-speed highway riding and spirited cornering. It is the perfect choice for riders starting their journey into the world of sports touring and long-distance exploration.

name: Suzuki Gixxer SF (155)
text: Suzuki Gixxer SF (155) is a sports bike. It has 155.0cc engine, gives 45 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.45 Lakh. The Suzuki Gixxer SF is a fully-faired version of the iconic Gixxer, offering sporty aesthetics and aerodynamic efficiency. It features a refined 155cc fuel-injected engine that produces 13.6 PS of power and 13.8 Nm of torque. The motorcycle can reach a top speed of 120 kmph, providing a stable and vibration-free ride on open roads. Its aerodynamic fairing is designed to reduce wind resistance and provide a premium, big-bike appearance. The bike is equipped with a fully digital instrument cluster that is clear and packed with information. It features a sharp LED headlamp and a sporty twin-outlet exhaust that provides a deep and rhythmic sound. The single-channel ABS and disc brakes at both ends ensure confident braking performance. It is built on a high-rigidity chassis that provides exceptional handling and stability during high-speed cornering. The split-seat and clip-on handlebars offer a balanced and sporty riding posture for both city and highway use. It is a stylish and reliable entry-level sportbike for fans of the Suzuki racing heritage.

name: Yamaha R15S
text: Yamaha R15S is a sports bike. It has 155.0cc engine, gives 45 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.66 Lakh. The Yamaha R15S is a more practical version of the R15 V3, designed with a single-piece seat for extra pillion comfort. It is powered by the high-revving 155cc engine with Variable Valve Actuation (VVA) technology, producing 18.6 PS and 14.1 Nm of torque. The motorcycle can reach a top speed of 140 kmph, maintaining the class-leading performance the R15 is famous for. It features an aerodynamic design inspired by Yamaha's larger R-series superbikes. The bike comes with a deltabox frame that provides legendary handling and stability during tight cornering. It is equipped with a dual-channel ABS system and large disc brakes at both ends for superior safety. The fully digital instrument cluster includes a shift timing light and several other race-inspired data readouts. Its liquid-cooling system ensures consistent performance even during long and high-speed highway journeys. The 6-speed gearbox with an assist and slipper clutch allows for effortless and smooth gear shifts. It is the perfect choice for riders who want track-level performance with a bit more everyday practicality for a passenger.

name: Bajaj Pulsar N125
text: Bajaj Pulsar N125 is a street bike. It has 124.0cc engine, gives 55 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.95 Lakh. The Bajaj Pulsar N125 is the latest addition to the 'N' series of the Pulsar family, designed as a modern urban commuter. It features an all-new 124.58cc engine that generates 12 PS of power and a peak torque of 11 Nm. The motorcycle is capable of reaching a top speed of 105 kmph, making it very effective for city navigation. It stands out with its aggressive streetfighter styling, featuring a sharp LED headlamp and a muscular fuel tank. The bike is equipped with a digital instrument cluster that offers Bluetooth connectivity for call and SMS alerts. For safety, it comes with a combined braking system (CBS) and a 240mm front disc brake. The light-weight chassis ensures that the bike remains agile and easy to flick through heavy traffic. It features a mono-shock rear suspension which is a premium touch for the 125cc segment. This model is perfect for young riders looking for a sporty look with high fuel efficiency.

name: Bajaj Freedom 125 (CNG)
text: Bajaj Freedom 125 (CNG) is a commuter bike. It has 125.0cc engine, gives 100 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.95 Lakh. The Bajaj Freedom 125 is the world's first production CNG motorcycle, representing a massive shift in the Indian commuter market. It is powered by a 125cc engine that produces 9.5 PS of power and 9.7 Nm of peak torque while running on CNG. The motorcycle can reach a top speed of 90 kmph, which is balanced for economic daily commuting. It features a dual-fuel system with a 2-kg CNG tank and a 2-liter petrol tank to eliminate range anxiety. The bike is designed with a long, flat seat that offers immense comfort for both rider and pillion. It includes a digital console with Bluetooth connectivity for modern convenience. The suspension consists of telescopic forks at the front and a linked mono-shock at the rear. Safety is handled by a front disc brake and a synchronized braking system. It is specifically targeted at high-mileage users who want to significantly reduce their fuel costs.

name: Bajaj Pulsar N250 (USD Update)
text: Bajaj Pulsar N250 (USD Update) is a sports bike. It has 249.0cc engine, gives 35 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.51 Lakh. The 2024 Bajaj Pulsar N250 brings significant technical upgrades to the quarter-liter naked streetfighter. It is powered by a 249cc oil-cooled engine that delivers 24.5 PS of maximum power and 21.5 Nm of peak torque. This allows the bike to hit a top speed of 135 kmph, providing a robust performance on highways and city roads. The most notable update is the inclusion of 37mm USD (Upside Down) front forks for improved front-end feel and stability. It now features a fully digital instrument cluster with turn-by-turn navigation and traction control system. The bike offers three ABS modes: Road, Rain, and Off-road, which is a segment-first feature. It retains its signature LED projector headlamp and DRLs that give it a menacing look. The assist and slipper clutch ensure smooth gear transitions during aggressive downshifts. With its wide 140-section rear tire, it provides excellent grip and confidence around corners.

name: Hero Xpulse 200 4V Pro
text: Hero Xpulse 200 4V Pro is a adventure bike. It has 199.0cc engine, gives 35 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.55 Lakh. The Hero Xpulse 200 4V Pro is the hard-core off-road focused version of the standard Xpulse. It features a 199.6cc 4-valve oil-cooled engine producing 19.1 PS of power and 17.35 Nm of torque. The motorcycle has a top speed of 115 kmph, but its true strength lies in its low-end grunt for tackling trails. The Pro variant comes with fully adjustable long-travel suspension at both ends, offering 250mm of ground clearance. It features an extended gear lever and a taller seat height of 891mm for professional-level off-roading. The bike is equipped with switchable ABS modes: Road, Off-road, and Rally, providing total control. It also includes an LED headlamp with increased intensity for better night trail riding. The digital instrument cluster provides turn-by-turn navigation through smartphone pairing. Its aluminum skid plate protects the engine during heavy landings in dirt.

name: Suzuki Gixxer 250 (2024)
text: Suzuki Gixxer 250 (2024) is a street bike. It has 249.0cc engine, gives 35 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.93 Lakh. The 2024 Suzuki Gixxer 250 is a muscular naked motorcycle designed for high-performance street riding. It is powered by a 249cc oil-cooled (SOCS) engine producing 26.5 PS of power and 22.2 Nm of peak torque. This motorcycle reaches a top speed of 150 kmph, making it a capable highway performer as well. The Suzuki Oil Cooling System ensures the engine remains compact and lightweight without the need for a liquid radiator. It features a sharp LED headlamp and a thick 41mm front fork setup for stable handling. The bike is equipped with dual-channel ABS as standard for maximum braking safety. It includes the Suzuki Easy Start system and a fully digital instrument cluster with gear position and shift indicators. The wide 140-section rear tire provides excellent stability during high-speed cornering. Its upright and slightly forward-leaning posture offers a perfect balance of sportiness and comfort.

name: Honda NX500
text: Honda NX500 is a touring bike. It has 471.0cc engine, gives 28 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 5.9 Lakh. The Honda NX500 is a premium adventure tourer launched to replace the CB500X in the Indian market. It is powered by a 471cc parallel-twin liquid-cooled engine producing 47.5 PS of power and 43 Nm of torque. The motorcycle can comfortably hit a top speed of 180 kmph, making it a serious machine for long-distance touring. It features a lightweight design with a 5-inch full-color TFT screen that supports smartphone connectivity. The bike is equipped with Honda Selectable Torque Control (HSTC) for safety on slippery surfaces. It has an upright riding position with a tall windscreen for excellent wind protection. The suspension setup consists of Showa SFF-BP USD forks and a pro-link monoshock for a plush ride quality. It uses dual-channel ABS with twin disc brakes at the front for immense stopping power. The NX500 is designed for riders who want a reliable, high-performance tourer capable of handling both highways and gravel paths.

name: TVS Jupiter 110 (2024)
text: TVS Jupiter 110 (2024) is a scooter bike. It has 113.0cc engine, gives 50 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.73 Lakh. The all-new TVS Jupiter 110 launched in 2024 is a complete redesign of India’s popular family scooter. It features a new 113.3cc engine with i-GO Assist technology that produces 8.02 PS of power and a peak torque of 9.8 Nm. The scooter can reach a top speed of 82 kmph, making it efficient for urban daily commutes. It stands out with its segment-first 'Infinite' LED light bar that spans across the front apron. The 'i-GO' tech provides a power boost during overtakes using the integrated starter generator. It offers a massive 33-liter under-seat storage space that can fit two helmets side-by-side. The scooter comes with a fully digital color console featuring turn-by-turn navigation and call alerts. For safety, it is equipped with a front disc brake and synchronized braking technology. This model is one of the most technologically advanced 110cc scooters currently available in India.

name: Honda Activa 6G (H-Smart)
text: Honda Activa 6G (H-Smart) is a scooter bike. It has 110.0cc engine, gives 50 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.82 Lakh. The Honda Activa 6G H-Smart is the most advanced version of India's best-selling scooter. It is powered by a reliable 109.51cc fuel-injected engine that produces 7.84 PS of power and 8.90 Nm of torque. The scooter reaches a top speed of 85 kmph, focusing on smoothness and high fuel efficiency. The H-Smart variant introduces a smart key system, allowing for keyless start, remote locking, and a 'find my scooter' feature. It includes an ACG starter for a silent start mechanism that enhances the premium feel. The scooter features a telescopic front suspension and a 3-step adjustable rear suspension for better ride quality. It has a durable full-metal body which is a signature trait of the Activa brand. The large 12-inch front wheel provides stability over varying Indian road conditions. This model remains a top choice for families due to its reliability and low maintenance costs.

name: Suzuki Access 125 (2024)
text: Suzuki Access 125 (2024) is a scooter bike. It has 124.0cc engine, gives 48 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.8 Lakh. The Suzuki Access 125 is a leading premium 125cc scooter in the Indian market known for its retro-modern design. It is powered by a 124cc air-cooled engine that delivers 8.7 PS of power and a peak torque of 10 Nm. The scooter has a top speed of 92 kmph, offering a punchy performance that is great for highway and city use. It features a sophisticated chrome-finished LED headlamp and a classic analog-digital instrument cluster. The latest version includes Bluetooth connectivity for navigation, call alerts, and ETA updates on the screen. It offers an external fuel-filler cap, which provides great convenience for riders during refueling. The seat is long and comfortable, making it ideal for carrying pillion passengers with ease. It comes with a front disc brake and CBS for safe stopping power. The Access 125 is highly regarded for its refined engine and smooth vibration-free ride.

name: Yamaha RayZR 125 Fi-Hybrid
text: Yamaha RayZR 125 Fi-Hybrid is a scooter bike. It has 125.0cc engine, gives 55 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.85 Lakh. The Yamaha RayZR 125 Fi-Hybrid is a lightweight and sporty scooter designed for the younger generation. It is powered by a 125cc air-cooled engine assisted by a Smart Motor Generator (SMG) system, producing 8.2 PS of power and 10.3 Nm of torque. The scooter reaches a top speed of 90 kmph and is known for being one of the lightest in its class at just 99kg. The hybrid system provides an extra power boost during acceleration from a standstill, improving fuel efficiency and pickup. It features a bold 'tough-looking' design with a brush guard and a sleek LED headlamp. The digital instrument cluster is compatible with the Y-Connect app to provide phone notifications and fuel tracking. It has a large 21-liter under-seat storage space for convenience. The telescopic front forks and wide tires ensure a stable and comfortable ride over city bumps.

name: Honda CB200X
text: Honda CB200X is a touring bike. It has 184.0cc engine, gives 40 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.47 Lakh. The Honda CB200X is an urban explorer designed for riders who enjoy both city commutes and weekend getaways. It is powered by an 184.4cc engine that produces 17.2 PS of power and 15.9 Nm of torque. The bike can comfortably reach a top speed of 120 kmph, offering a stable ride on highways. It features an upright riding position and a tall windscreen to reduce wind blast during longer rides. The design is inspired by larger adventure bikes, featuring knuckle guards with integrated LED indicators. It is equipped with USD front forks and a mono-shock rear suspension for superior damping. The safety features include a 276mm front disc with single-channel ABS. It also features a fully digital liquid crystal meter that displays the gear position and clock. The engine is refined and provides a linear power delivery typical of Honda motorcycles.

name: TVS Ntorq 125 (XT)
text: TVS Ntorq 125 (XT) is a scooter bike. It has 124.0cc engine, gives 42 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.03 Lakh. The TVS Ntorq 125 XT is the most tech-loaded variant of India's favorite performance scooter. It is powered by a 124.8cc 3-valve engine that produces 9.38 PS of power and a peak torque of 10.5 Nm. The scooter can reach a top speed of 95 kmph, offering one of the fastest acceleration figures in the 125cc segment. It features a unique hybrid TFT and LCD instrument cluster that provides social media alerts and weather updates. The SmartXonnect system offers voice assistance and turn-by-turn navigation with food and fuel station discovery. It comes with a distinct set of lightweight alloy wheels and a neon color scheme to stand out. The 'IntelliGO' technology helps in saving fuel by automatically switching off the engine during long idles at traffic lights. It has a high-quality disc brake at the front for sharp and reliable stopping power.

name: Suzuki Avenis 125 (2024)
text: Suzuki Avenis 125 (2024) is a scooter bike. It has 124.0cc engine, gives 48 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 0.92 Lakh. The 2024 Suzuki Avenis 125 is a sporty commuter scooter that competes directly in the high-performance 125cc segment. It is equipped with a 124.3cc engine producing 8.7 PS of power and 10 Nm of torque. The scooter reaches a top speed of 92 kmph, providing a snappy response for quick overtakes in the city. It features an aggressive bodywork with sharp lines and a split-type LED tail lamp. The digital console is Bluetooth-enabled and connects via the Suzuki Ride Connect app for navigation and call alerts. It includes an external fuel filler cap and a large under-seat storage space for added practicality. The scooter also features a USB charging socket in the front pocket to keep your devices charged on the go. Its light-weight design at 106kg ensures easy handling for riders of all skill levels.

name: Yamaha Aerox 155 (Version S)
text: Yamaha Aerox 155 (Version S) is a scooter bike. It has 155.0cc engine, gives 40 kmpl mileage. Price is usually 1.51 Lakh. The Yamaha Aerox 155 Version S is India’s most powerful maxi-style scooter, sharing its engine with the R15.
